desc: Hell-Ville is the transport and market hub of Nosy Be, used as a gateway to beaches, marine parks, and seasonal whale-shark excursions.
hls: Base for Nosy Tanikely and Nosy Komba boat trips
Dry-season beach weather and clearer water
Access point for whale-shark season excursions

todo.name: Hell-Ville market morning
todo.desc: Browse spice stalls, vanilla products, and fresh seafood auctions.

todo.name: Nosy Tanikely snorkel trip
todo.desc: Take a half-day boat for reef snorkeling and marine reserve beaches.

todo.name: Mont Passot sunset
todo.desc: Head inland for crater-lake and ocean panoramas at golden hour.

todo.name: Nosy Komba lemur visit
todo.desc: Combine village craft stops with forest lemur sightings.

todo.name: Lokobe Reserve pirogue tour
todo.desc: Use a guided paddle route for endemic reptiles and lemurs.

todo.name: Beach day at Andilana
todo.desc: Set aside one full day for swimming and shoreline downtime.

wifi.note: Usable in town hotels; island-wide outages and power cuts are common.

prac.fltNote: UK/Ireland trips are usually one-stop via Addis Ababa, Nairobi, or Paris plus a regional leg; Nosy Be capacity peaks in the dry season (June-October), when fares and lodge rates rise.
prac.currency: Madagascar local currency (check live rates before booking).
prac.lang: Local language plus tourism-level English in main visitor areas.
prac.tz: Check local timezone and DST rules for exact travel dates.
prac.visa: Confirm current entry/transit rules for your passport before booking.
alerts: Boat transfers can be cancelled on windy days in July-August; keep marine excursions flexible.
ATMs are limited and can run out of cash on weekends, so withdraw before heading to outer beaches.
Road surfaces degrade quickly after rain; night transfers from remote lodges are slower than map estimates.

sweet: Jun-Oct for driest weather, calmer sea days, and best snorkeling visibility.
